title: verbascum lagurus subsp. ponticum
intro: Verbascum lagurus subsp. ponticum, also known as Pontic mullein, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mullein genus, characterized by its tall spikes of yellow flowers and downy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Pontic mullein include its potential to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
